title: Fix MongoDB Pipeline to Handle Nested Fields with Mixed Types and Optional Keys

statement.markdown: You are given a MongoDB collection with documents that have the following structure. You need to write a function that takes a MongoDB pipeline and a list of field names as input, and returns a new pipeline that ignores embedded fields with mixed types and optional keys in the projection stage.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a MongoDB pipeline and a list of field names.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a new MongoDB pipeline.
statement.notes: The function should ignore embedded fields with mixed types and optional keys in the projection stage.

constraints.description: Number of documents in the MongoDB collection

examples.explanation: The function should ignore the 'address' field in the second document because it has a country field which is not present in the first document.

hints.text: Use the $type operator to check the type of each field in the projection stage.
Use the $ifNull operator to check if a field is null in the projection stage.
Use the $ne operator to check if a field is not equal to an empty string in the projection stage.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fix MongoDB Pipeline to Handle Nested Fields with Mixed Types and Optional Keys
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to use the $type operator to check the type of each field in the projection stage, and the $ifNull operator to check if a field is null. We can then use the $ne operator to check if a field is not equal to an empty string.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would be to use a recursive function to iterate over each field in the document and check its type and existence.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use the $type operator and the $ifNull operator to simplify the pipeline.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a single pipeline that checks the type and existence of each field in the projection stage.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correct pipeline will ignore embedded fields with mixed types and optional keys, and will return the expected output.
editorial.dryRun: You can test the pipeline with the following documents:

testCases.input: [ { name: 'John', age: 30, address: { street: '123 Main St', city: 'Anytown', state: 'CA', zip: '12345' } }, { name: 'Jane', age: 25, address: { street: '456 Elm St', city: 'Othertown', country: 'USA' } } ]

[ { name: 'John', age: 30, address: { street: '123 Main St', city: 'Anytown', state: 'CA', zip: '12345' } }, { name: 'Jane', age: 25, address: { street: '456 Elm St', city: 'Othertown', country: 'USA' } } ]
